instruction:
Let the random variables $( \mathbf{X}, \ \mathbf{Y} )$ follow a two-dimensional normal distribution $\mathrm{N} \ ( 0, \ 0 ; \ 1, \ 4 ; \ -1 / 2 )$. Which of the following random variables follows a standard normal distribution and is independent of $X$?

Options:
A. ${\frac{1}{2}} ( X + 2Y )$
B. ${\frac{\sqrt{5}} {5}} ( X+Y )$
C. ${\frac{1}{\sqrt{7}}} ( 3X - Y )$
D. ${\frac{\sqrt{2}} {2}} ( X - Y )$
E. ${\frac{\sqrt{3}} {3}} ( X-Y )$
F. ${\frac{\sqrt{6}} {6}} ( 2X + Y )$
G. ${\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}} ( X + 3Y )$
H. ${\frac{\sqrt{5}} {5}} ( X-Y )$
I. ${\frac{\sqrt{3}} {3}} ( X+Y )$
J. ${\frac{1}{2}} ( 2X - Y )$
